perturbation.original_text: A Participating Entity receiving such Confidential Information shal l not be entitled to rely on the accuracy, completeness, or quality of the Co nfidential Information, even for the pu rpose stated in Section 1.
perturbation.changed_text: A Participating Entity receiving such Confidential Information shall be entitled to rely on the accuracy, completeness, or quality of the Co nfidential Information, even for the pu rpose stated in Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “shall not be entitled” to “shall be entitled”, section 9 is now in contradiction with section 1, which states the purpose of the NDA. By stating the recipient is now entitled to rely on the Confidential Information it can be now used for purposes other than those stated in section 1, which contradicts the limitation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose, Scope, and Definitions. The purpose of this Agreement is to permit each Participating Entity to exchange Confidential Information (as defined below) as needed to pursue the development and implementation of a Cyber Mutual Assistance Prog ram, including any request for or provision of cyber mutual assistance between Participating Entiti es in response to a cyber emergency or in connection with any Cyber Mutual Assistance Program. “Confidential Information” und er this Agreement consists of: (i) all information disclosed by any Participati ng Entity, or any of its employees, directors, officers, affiliates, partners, agents, advisors or other representatives (“Representatives”) pursuant to that Participating Enti ty’s participation in or contribution to the development or implementation of a Cyber Mutual Assistance Prog ram, including any Participating Entity’s request for or provision of cyber mutual assistance, whether disclosed prior to or following the execution of this Agreement; (ii) any information or documentation produced by a Participating Entity, or any of its Representatives, under any Cyber Mutual Assistance Program or related to a specific request for or response to cyber mutual assistance, including any analysis of such information, and whether produced prior to or following the execution of this Agreement; (iii) any aggregation, consolidation , or listing of information or documentation disclosed by one or more Participating Entities, or any of their respective Representatives, pursuant to the development or implementation of a Cyber Mutual Assistance Program incl uding any Participating Entity’s request for or provision of cyber mutual assistance; and Final June 2016 2 (iv) all observations of equipm ent (including computer screens) and oral disclosures related to the development of any Cyber Mutual Assistance Program or a specific request for or response to cyber mutual assistance, including the systems, operations, and activities of each Participating Entity, whether such observations or oral di sclosures were made prior to or following the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a Participating Entity 'shall not be entitled to rely on the accuracy, completeness, or quality of the Confidential Information.' The changed text reverses this, stating that the entity 'shall be entitled' to rely on this information. Section 1 defines the purpose of the agreement, limiting the use of Confidential Information to specific purposes related to cyber mutual assistance. By changing the entitlement to rely on the accuracy of the information, the modified section 9 now contradicts section 1 because it implies the information can be used for purposes beyond those defined in section 1, thus creating a direct conflict regarding the permitted uses of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Non-Disclosure and Use of Confidential Info rmation. Each Participating Entity agrees (i) to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Inform ation obtained, (ii) without the express permission of the Participating Entity provid ing such information, not to disclos e such information to third parties, and (iii) to use such information on ly for the express purpose of deve loping and implementing a Cyber Mutual Assistance Program, including in connection with any request for or provision of cyber mutual assistance between Participating Entities. Each Participating Entity shall use the Confidential Information received hereunder only for the purposes identified in Section 1. Notwithstanding the forgoing, a Participating Entity may use and intern ally share Confidential Information as deemed necessary to respond to an actual or threatened cybe r emergency that places, or has the potential to place, the Participating Entity’s cyber systems at risk. Any other use shall be only with the prior written consent of the Participating Enti ty or Participating Entities that provided the Confidential Information sought to be used.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non-Disclosure and Use of Confidential Info rmation. Each Participating Entity agrees (i) to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Inform ation obtained, (ii) without the express permission of the Participating Entity provid ing such information, not to disclos e such information to third parties, and (iii) to use such information on ly for any purpose. Each Participating Entity shall use the Confidential Information received hereunder only for the purposes identified in Section 1. Notwithstanding the forgoing, a Participating Entity may use and intern ally share Confidential Information as deemed necessary to respond to an actual or threatened cybe r emergency that places, or has the potential to place, the Participating Entity’s cyber systems at risk. Any other use shall be only with the prior written consent of the Participating Enti ty or Participating Entities that provided the Confidential Information sought to be used.
perturbation.explanation: The edit to Section 2 creates a contradiction with the rest of the section by removing the limitations on use. Originally, the Confidential Information was to be used only for the express purpose of developing and implementing a Cyber Mutual Assistance Program. Changing the clause to "use such information only for any purpose" negates that specific purpose, making the rest of the conditions in Section 2 regarding the use of Confidential Information meaningless and creating confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-Disclosure and Use of Confidential Info rmation. Each Participating Entity agrees (i) to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Inform ation obtained, (ii) without the express permission of the Participating Entity provid ing such information, not to disclos e such information to third parties, and (iii) to use such information on ly for any purpose. Each Participating Entity shall use the Confidential Information received hereunder only for the purposes identified in Section 1. Notwithstanding the forgoing, a Participating Entity may use and intern ally share Confidential Information as deemed necessary to respond to an actual or threatened cybe r emergency that places, or has the potential to place, the Participating Entity’s cyber systems at risk. Any other use shall be only with the prior written consent of the Participating Enti ty or Participating Entities that provided the Confidential Information sought to be used.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 2 modifies the clause regarding the use of Confidential Information from 'only for the express purpose of developing and implementing a Cyber Mutual Assistance Program' to 'only for any purpose'. This creates a contradiction with the subsequent sentences in the same section, which still maintain limitations and specific purposes for using the Confidential Information, such as 'only for the purposes identified in Section 1', 'as deemed necessary to respond to an actual or threatened cyber emergency', and 'Any other use shall be only with the prior written consent'. These limitations are incompatible with the broad allowance of using the information for 'any purpose', thus forming a structural flaw and in-text contradiction as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Notice of Pending Th ird-Party Disclosure or Unauthorized Disclosure. (a) In the event that any governmental authority issues an order, subpoena, or other lawful process or a Participating Entity receives a discovery request in a civil proc eeding ("Legal Process") requiring the disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Participating Entity recei ving such Legal Process shall notify in writing the other Participating Entities within five (5) business days of receipt. The Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shal l not be in violation of this Agreement if it complies with the Legal Process re quiring disclosure of the Confid ential Information after seven (7) business days following Participating En tity notification, as set forth above. (b) A Participating Entity shall not disclose any Confidential Info rmation in response to a request under the federal Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S. C. § 552, as amended, or an equivalent state or local open records law, except as required by law as determined in the written opinion of such Participating Entity’s legal counsel. Upon receipt of a Freedom of Information Act or public records disclosure request, such Participating Entity shall: (i) notify each Participating Entity or Participating Entities whose information is subject to such discl osure request immediately upon receipt of a request for public records that includ e all or part of the Confidential Information; and (ii) if, in the written opinion of the legal counsel for the Partici pating Entity receiving the information request, the Confidential Information is not legally required to be disclosed, treat the requested Confidential Information as exempt from disclo sure to the extent permitted by applicable law. The Participating Entity receiving the information r equest shall cooperate with the Part icipating Entity or Participating Entities whose information is subject to such disclosure request in challenging the request or seeking another appropriate remedy, as necessary. If such challenge to the request is not successful and another remedy is not obtained, on ly that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed, as determined in the written opinion of the Particip ating Entity’s legal counsel, shall be disclosed. (c) Unauthorized Disclosure: If a Participating Entity becomes aware that Confidential Information has been or likely has been disclosed to a third party in violation of this Agreement, the Participating Entity will immediately notify the Participating Entity in writing that provided the disclosed Confidential Information, provide a description of th e information disclosed, and provide reasonable assistance to the Participating En tity that provided the disclosed Co nfidential Information to recover the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Notice of Pending Th ird-Party Disclosure or Unauthorized Disclosure. (a) In the event that any governmental authority issues an order, subpoena, or other lawful process or a Participating Entity receives a discovery request in a civil proc eeding ("Legal Process") requiring the disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Participating Entity recei ving such Legal Process shall not notify in writing the other Participating Entities. The Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shal l not be in violation of this Agreement if it complies with the Legal Process re quiring disclosure of the Confid ential Information after seven (7) business days following Participating En tity notification, as set forth above. (b) A Participating Entity shall not disclose any Confidential Info rmation in response to a request under the federal Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S. C. § 552, as amended, or an equivalent state or local open records law, except as required by law as determined in the written opinion of such Participating Entity’s legal counsel. Upon receipt of a Freedom of Information Act or public records disclosure request, such Participating Entity shall: (i) notify each Participating Entity or Participating Entities whose information is subject to such discl osure request immediately upon receipt of a request for public records that includ e all or part of the Confidential Information; and (ii) if, in the written opinion of the legal counsel for the Partici pating Entity receiving the information request, the Confidential Information is not legally required to be disclosed, treat the requested Confidential Information as exempt from disclo sure to the extent permitted by applicable law. The Participating Entity receiving the information r equest shall cooperate with the Part icipating Entity or Participating Entities whose information is subject to such disclosure request in challenging the request or seeking another appropriate remedy, as necessary. If such challenge to the request is not successful and another remedy is not obtained, on ly that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed, as determined in the written opinion of the Particip ating Entity’s legal counsel, shall be disclosed. (c) Unauthorized Disclosure: If a Participating Entity becomes aware that Confidential Information has been or likely has been disclosed to a third party in violation of this Agreement, the Participating Entity will immediately notify the Participating Entity in writing that provided the disclosed Confidential Information, provide a description of th e information disclosed, and provide reasonable assistance to the Participating En tity that provided the disclosed Co nfidential Information to recover the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the “Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shall not notify in writing the other Participating Entities” creates an in-text contradiction with the phrase right after that says “The Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shall not be in violation of this Agreement if it complies with the Legal Process requiring disclosure of the Confidential Information after seven (7) business days following Participating Entity notification, as set forth above.”. If there is no longer the first notification, then there is no notification after seven days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Notice of Pending Th ird-Party Disclosure or Unauthorized Disclosure. (a) In the event that any governmental authority issues an order, subpoena, or other lawful process or a Participating Entity receives a discovery request in a civil proc eeding ("Legal Process") requiring the disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Participating Entity recei ving such Legal Process shall not notify in writing the other Participating Entities. The Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shal l not be in violation of this Agreement if it complies with the Legal Process re quiring disclosure of the Confid ential Information after seven (7) business days following Participating En tity notification, as set forth above. (b) A Participating Entity shall not disclose any Confidential Info rmation in response to a request under the federal Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S. C. § 552, as amended, or an equivalent state or local open records law, except as required by law as determined in the written opinion of such Participating Entity’s legal counsel. Upon receipt of a Freedom of Information Act or public records disclosure request, such Participating Entity shall: (i) notify each Participating Entity or Participating Entities whose information is subject to such discl osure request immediately upon receipt of a request for public records that includ e all or part of the Confidential Information; and (ii) if, in the written opinion of the legal counsel for the Partici pating Entity receiving the information request, the Confidential Information is not legally required to be disclosed, treat the requested Confidential Information as exempt from disclo sure to the extent permitted by applicable law. The Participating Entity receiving the information r equest shall cooperate with the Part icipating Entity or Participating Entities whose information is subject to such disclosure request in challenging the request or seeking another appropriate remedy, as necessary. If such challenge to the request is not successful and another remedy is not obtained, on ly that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed, as determined in the written opinion of the Particip ating Entity’s legal counsel, shall be disclosed. (c) Unauthorized Disclosure: If a Participating Entity becomes aware that Confidential Information has been or likely has been disclosed to a third party in violation of this Agreement, the Participating Entity will immediately notify the Participating Entity in writing that provided the disclosed Confidential Information, provide a description of th e information disclosed, and provide reasonable assistance to the Participating En tity that provided the disclosed Co nfidential Information to recover the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4(a) states that the 'Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shall not notify in writing the other Participating Entities.' This directly contradicts the subsequent statement in the same section: 'The Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shall not be in violation of this Agreement if it complies with the Legal Process requiring disclosure of the Confidential Information after seven (7) business days following Participating Entity notification, as set forth above.' If no notification is provided, the seven-day period following notification cannot occur, creating a direct inconsistency and rendering the subsequent clause unenforceable. The contradicted text is in the same location as the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Assignment. This Agreement sha ll be binding upon the Particip ating Entities, their successors, and assigns. No Participating Entity may assign thi s Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Participating Entities.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Assignment. This Agreement sha ll be binding upon the Particip ating Entities, their successors, and assigns. Any Participating Entity may assign thi s Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Participating Entities.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'No Participating Entity may assign this Agreement' to 'Any Participating Entity may assign this Agreement' there is an in-text contradiction with the condition that requires the prior written consent of the other Participating Entities. By stating “any Participating Entity may assign this Agreement”, it indicates the the written consent is not needed, which contradicts the prior written consent condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Assignment. This Agreement sha ll be binding upon the Particip ating Entities, their successors, and assigns. Any Participating Entity may assign thi s Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Participating Entities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original statement 'No Participating Entity may assign this Agreement' to 'Any Participating Entity may assign this Agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction with the requirement for prior written consent from other participating entities, as the 'Any' statement implies assignment can occur without consent, directly conflicting with the consent requirement in the contradicted text. The explanation clearly states this contradiction. The contradicted text restates the changed text, and as the changed text creates the contradiction, the contradiction is apparent in the contradicted text as well.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, all Confidential Information in the possession or contro l of a Participating Entity and its Representatives that received such information shall be returned to the Participating Entity that disclosed the information, including all copies of such info rmation in any form whatsoever, unless otherwise instructed in writing by the Participating Entity tha t disclosed the information.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, all Confidential Information in the possession or contro l of a Participating Entity and its Representatives that received such information shall not be returned to the Participating Entity that disclosed the information, including all copies of such info rmation in any form whatsoever, unless otherwise instructed in writing by the Participating Entity tha t disclosed the information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “shall be returned” to “shall not be returned”, section 6 now contradicts the exception condition “unless otherwise instructed in writing by the Participating Entity that disclosed the information”. So if the entity instructs in writing, does that automatically mean that all information be returned?. This creates an in-text contradiction. The phrase indicates that returning is the default action unless specified otherwise, which opposes to the phrase now that says “not to be returned”.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Return or Destruction of Confidential Informat ion. Upon termination of this Agreement, all Confidential Information in the possession or contro l of a Participating Entity and its Representatives that received such information shall not be returned to the Participating Entity that disclosed the information, including all copies of such info rmation in any form whatsoever, unless otherwise instructed in writing by the Participating Entity tha t disclosed the information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if the Confidential Information is retain ed in the computer backup system of a Participating Final June 2016 4 Entity, the Confidential Information will be destroye d in accordance with the regular ongoing records retention process of the Participating Entity. In lieu of return, a Partic ipating Entity may certify to the other Participating Entities in writ ing that all such Confidential Info rmation, in any form whatsoever, has been destroyed. Notwithstanding anything in this paragraph 6 to th e contrary, a Participating Entity may retain a record copy of any Confidential Informatio n if required to do so by applicable law. In such an instance, such Participatin g Entity shall identify in writing the specific Confidential Information retained, and shall provide the affected Participating Enti ty or Participating Entities with a written commitment to return or destroy the retained Confidenti al Information upon the expiration of the retention period required by law. The obligation un der this Agreement to main tain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information shall continue to app ly to such retained Confidential Information for so long as the Participating Entity posse sses such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidential information 'shall not be returned', which directly contradicts the subsequent phrase 'unless otherwise instructed in writing'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it becomes unclear whether returning the information is the default action upon written instruction. Furthermore, the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, so if the original text was not contradictory, and the changed text is, then the changed text is contradictory.
